title: torilis arvensis subsp. turcomanica
intro: Torilis arvensis subsp. turcomanica, also known as Turkestan knotted hedge parsley, is a plant species that belongs to the carrot family (Apiaceae) and is native to Central Asia and the Caucasus region. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, making it useful in treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Turkestan knotted hedge parsley include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. It can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat digestive issues, fever, and other ailments.
